data.name: PermBuilderInvalid
data.value: Not a valid rule yet — complete the fields above.

data.name: PermBuilderBareWildcardPath
data.value: A path of only wildcards matches every file. Leave the path empty to allow all paths for this tool, or enter a specific pattern.

data.name: PermBuilderGlossInvalid
data.value: Fill in the fields to see what this rule will match.

data.name: PermBuilderAddedFmt
data.comment: Transient builder confirmation. {0} = rule string, {1} = bucket (Allow/Ask/Deny).

data.name: PermBuilderCollisionConflict
data.value: ⚠ Conflicts with `{0}` in {1} — deny/ask takes precedence over allow.
data.comment: Cross-bucket collision note. {0} = existing rule, {1} = its bucket.

data.name: PermBuilderCollisionRedundant
data.value: Overlaps with `{0}` already in {1} — one may be redundant.
data.comment: Same-bucket subsumption note. {0} = existing rule, {1} = its bucket.

data.name: PermBuilderGlossShellAll
data.value: Matches ALL commands for this tool — very broad. Prefer a specific command.

data.name: PermBuilderGlossPathAll
data.value: Matches ALL file paths for this tool.

data.name: PermBuilderGlossWebDomain
data.value: Matches web fetches to `{0}` and its subdomains.

data.name: PermBuilderGlossMcpServer
data.value: Matches every tool from the MCP server `{0}`

data.name: PermBuilderGlossMcpTool
data.value: Matches the MCP tool `{0}` on server `{1}`.

data.name: PermTesterExplainDefault
data.value: No rule matched. The outcome falls through to the default mode: {0}.

data.name: PermTesterExplainMatchedScoped
data.value: Matched a {0} rule in the {2} scope: {1}

data.name: PermTesterReadOnlyNote
data.value: Note: Claude Code treats {0} as a read-only command and runs it without prompting, regardless of these rules.

data.name: PermTesterSubcommandWarning
data.value: Caution: this command embeds another command (via $(…), backticks, or a subshell). The verdict above reflects only the outer command — the embedded one runs but is not matched here, so a real run may be more permissive than shown.

data.name: PermEduIntro
data.value: Permission rules tell Claude Code which tool actions to allow, ask about, or deny. Each rule is either a bare tool name (matching every use of that tool) or `Tool(specifier)` for fine-grained control.

data.name: PermEduPrecedenceBody
data.value: Rules are evaluated `deny` → `ask` → `allow`, and the first match wins. A deny in ANY scope beats an allow in any scope. If no rule matches, the default mode decides.

data.name: PermEduPerToolHeading
data.value: Each tool has its own pattern style

data.name: PermEduPerToolBody
data.value: Bash and PowerShell use glob patterns: `*` matches anything, even spaces, and a space before a trailing `*` forces a word boundary (so `ls *` matches `ls -la` but not `lsof`). Read/Edit/Write use gitignore-style paths with four anchors: `//absolute`, `~/home`, `/project-root`, and `./current-directory`. WebFetch uses `domain:host`. MCP uses `mcp__server` or `mcp__server__tool`.

data.name: PermEduMistake1
data.value: • Expecting an allow rule to override a deny — it never does. Deny always wins.

data.name: PermEduMistake2
data.value: • Using the wrong path anchor: a single leading slash (`/path`) means your PROJECT ROOT, not the filesystem root. Use `//path` for an absolute path.

data.name: PermEduMistake3
data.value: • Trying to constrain Bash arguments. Patterns like `Bash(curl example.com *)` are fragile — option order, redirects, and variables defeat them. For network control, deny `curl` / `wget` and allow `WebFetch(domain:…)` instead.

data.name: PermBuilderHintWebDomain
data.value: Matches the host `example.com` and all its subdomains

data.name: PermBuilderHintGroupWildcards
data.comment: Caption for the wildcard group of builder hints.

data.name: PermBuilderHintGroupAnchors
data.comment: Caption for the path-anchor group of builder hints.

data.name: PermBuilderToolTip
data.value: Choose which tool this rule applies to.

data.name: PermBuilderCommandTip
data.value: The command to match. Leave empty to match every command for this tool (very broad).

data.name: PermBuilderPrefixTip
data.value: When on, the rule matches this command followed by any arguments (appends a trailing wildcard).

data.name: PermBuilderPathTip
data.value: A file or folder path. Anchors: //absolute, ~/home, /project-root, or a plain/relative path from the current directory.

data.name: PermBuilderRecursiveTip
data.value: When on, matches all files under the folder (appends /**).

data.name: PermBuilderDomainTip
data.value: A domain such as example.com. Subdomains are included.

data.name: PermBuilderMcpServerTip
data.value: Choose the MCP server this rule applies to.

data.name: PermBuilderMcpToolTip
data.value: The specific MCP tool to match. Ignored when 'All tools' is on.

data.name: PermBuilderAgentTip
data.value: The subagent to match, e.g. Explore.

data.name: PermBuilderPreviewTip
data.value: The exact rule string that will be added.

data.name: PermBuilderAddAllowTip
data.value: Add this rule to the allow list at the current scope.

data.name: PermBuilderAddAskTip
data.value: Add this rule to the ask list at the current scope.

data.name: PermBuilderAddDenyTip
data.value: Add this rule to the deny list at the current scope.

data.name: PermTesterMergedTip
data.value: When on, evaluates rules from every scope together. When off, only the scope you are editing.

data.name: PermTesterNoInput
data.value: Enter a value above (a command, path, URL, MCP tool, or subagent) to see the decision Claude Code would make.

data.name: PermTesterScopeLabel
data.comment: Label for the dedicated scope callout in the tester result (merged view).

data.name: PermTesterInputTip
data.value: Describe what Claude would do; the result shows the decision Claude Code would make.
